questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Election Commission of India is a constitutional body.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is established under Article 324 of the Indian Constitution.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India is indeed a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Indian Constitution, making option A and R true and R the correct explanation of A.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The right to vote is a fundamental right.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression, which includes the right to vote.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The right to vote is actually a constitutional right, but it is guaranteed under Article 326, not Article 19(1)(a) which guarantees freedom of speech and expression. Hence, option A is true but R is false.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Electoral College in the United States is based on a proportional representation system.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Each state in the US has a certain number of electoral votes based on its population.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: While it is true that each state in the US has a certain number of electoral votes based on its population (R), the Electoral College is actually based on a winner-takes-all system, not a proportional representation system (A). Hence, option A is false but R is true.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Proportional representation is a method of election in which seats are allocated to parties based on the percentage of votes they receive.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This method is used in the European Parliament elections.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Proportional representation indeed allocates seats to parties based on the percentage of votes they receive, and it is used in the European Parliament elections. Hence, both option A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of 'One Person, One Vote' is used in the United States.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This concept was used in the landmark case of Reynolds v. Sims in 1964.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The concept of 'One Person, One Vote' is indeed used in the United States, and it was used in the landmark case of Reynolds v. Sims in 1964. Hence, both option A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Indian Constitution guarantees universal adult franchise.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Article 326 of the Indian Constitution ensures that every citizen above the age of 18 has the right to vote.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution indeed guarantees universal adult franchise, and Article 326 of the Indian Constitution ensures that every citizen above the age of 18 has the right to vote. Hence, both option A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of 'First Past the Post' is used in the United States.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This concept is used in the presidential elections of the United States.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The concept of 'First Past the Post' is actually used in the UK parliamentary elections, not the presidential elections of the United States. Hence, option A is false but R is true.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Election Commission of India is headed by a Chief Election Commissioner who is assisted by two Election Commissioners.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is in accordance with Article 324 of the Indian Constitution.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India is indeed headed by a Chief Election Commissioner who is assisted by two Election Commissioners, and this is in accordance with Article 324 of the Indian Constitution. Hence, both option A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of 'Single Transferable Vote' is used in the presidential elections of the United States.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This concept is used in the Irish presidential elections.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Single Transferable Vote' is actually used in some Australian and Irish elections, not the presidential elections of the United States. Hence, option A is false but R is true.
